new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch Whisky in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 30 79)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältern > 2 L (KN 2208 30 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch whisky — a spirit distilled from a mash of cereals, distilled and matured in Scotland, comprising a blend of malt and grain whiskies — marketed at an alcoholic strength by volume of 40% vol or higher, presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, without the addition of aerated water.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria are covered: (1) Blended Scotch Whisky identity (subclass of BlendedScotchWhisky, which is a subclass of Whisky); (2) Scotland origin via ScotlandOriginQuality; (3) minimum 40% vol alcoholic strength via MinAlcohol40VolQuality; (4) large container (> 2 L) via LargeContainer (TBox-defined as packaging with volume ≥ 2 L — note the CN boundary is > 2 L whereas the TBox uses ≥ 2 L, a minor boundary mismatch that cannot be resolved without a new LargeContainer class for strictly > 2 L); (5) distillation from cereal mash via WhiskyDistillationProcess and CerealMash part; (6) blending via BlendingProcess; (7) exclusion of aerated water (whisky-soda) via complement on AddedSodaWaterQuality. A small gap remains: the container volume boundary is > 2 L (strict) in the CN but the existing TBox LargeContainer uses ≥ 2 L (inclusive); a new class for strictly-greater-than-2-L containers would be needed for full precision, but following the narrow-over-broad rule the existing TBox class is reused. The blending criterion for Blended Scotch Whisky (blend of malt and grain whiskies) is partially captured through the parent class hierarchy (BlendedScotchWhisky) rather than explicit axioms, which is an acceptable modelling choice.
